eras.name: Han Dynasty Golden Age
eras.description: Defined by the establishment of the Silk Road, fashion focused on cross-collar robes (Shenyi) with wide sleeves and layered silk garments reflecting social hierarchy.
eras.aiImagePrompt: A noblewoman of the Han Dynasty wearing a multi-layered silk Quju robe with intricate cloud patterns, wide sleeves, and a high waist sash, set against a palace garden backdrop, cinematic lighting.

eras.name: Tang Dynasty Cosmopolitanism
eras.description: An era of unprecedented openness and opulence, featuring high-waisted skirts (Ruqun) and low-cut necklines influenced by Central Asian aesthetics.
eras.aiImagePrompt: A Tang Dynasty lady with elaborate floral headpieces, wearing a high-waisted silk skirt and a sheer silk wrap-around shawl, vibrant crimson and gold colors, traditional makeup with flower petal forehead markings.

eras.name: Qing Dynasty Manchu Influence
eras.description: Characterized by the introduction of the horseshoe sleeve and the evolution of the Pipa jacket, blending Manchu equestrian practicality with Han Chinese silk embroidery.
eras.aiImagePrompt: A high-ranking Qing Dynasty official in a formal silk robe with detailed dragon embroidery, horseshoe-shaped sleeves, and a jade-encrusted belt, historical accuracy, studio lighting.

eras.name: Republican Era Shanghai Modernism
eras.description: The birth of the modern body-conforming Qipao in Shanghai, merging traditional Chinese motifs with Western tailoring, high slits, and Art Deco influences.
eras.aiImagePrompt: A 1930s Shanghai socialite in a fitted silk Qipao with a high collar and geometric Art Deco patterns, standing near a vintage gramophone, noir aesthetic.

eras.name: Mao Era Uniformity
eras.description: A period of utilitarianism where fashion was political, dominated by the Zhongshan suit in drab colors to promote egalitarianism and revolutionary spirit.
eras.aiImagePrompt: A group of workers in 1960s Beijing wearing identical blue and olive green Mao suits with red star caps, socialist realism art style, propaganda poster aesthetic.

eras.name: The New Chinese Style (Guochao)
eras.description: A resurgence of national pride where young designers blend Hanfu elements with high-tech fabrics and streetwear silhouettes, known as the Guochao movement.
eras.aiImagePrompt: A modern street style photo in Shanghai featuring a youth wearing a deconstructed Hanfu-style techwear jacket with neon accents and traditional silk embroidery, futuristic urban background.

textiles.name: Yunbrocade (Yunjin)
textiles.description: Known as 'cloud brocade,' it uses gold and silver threads to create intricate, multi-colored patterns that are as light as clouds.
textiles.technique: Hand-weaving on a large wooden treadle loom

textiles.name: Gambiered Canton Gauze
textiles.description: A sustainable, mud-coated silk that is naturally dyed with yam juice and iron-rich river mud, resulting in a leather-like texture.

textiles.name: Su Embroidery
textiles.description: Famous for its delicacy and split-hair thin threads, often featuring double-sided designs where the front and back are identical.

textiles.name: Xiangyunsha
textiles.description: A traditional silk fabric that undergoes a complex fermentation process, resulting in a breathable, antibacterial textile used for summer wear.

climate.fashionImplication: The humid south favors breathable silks and linens with loose silhouettes, while the frigid north necessitates heavy padding, fur linings, and layered technical outerwear.

culturalInfluences: Silk Road Exchange (Persian and Central Asian motifs)

notableEvents: Met Gala: China Through the Looking Glass
